purpose.summary: The PAUDBLOD program reads root segment records from INFILE1 and child segment records from INFILE2, then inserts them into an IMS database. It reads the root segment file until the end of file is reached, then reads the child segment file until its end of file is reached, inserting each record into the IMS database.

inputs.name: INFILE1
inputs.description: Contains root segment records to be inserted into the IMS database as PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY.

inputs.name: INFILE2
inputs.description: Contains child segment records to be inserted into the IMS database as PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS.

inputs.name: PAUTBPCB
inputs.description: PCB (Program Communication Block) used for IMS database communication.

called_programs.purpose: Inserts root and child segments into the IMS database.

transforms.transformation_description: Moves the content of the input record to the pending authorization summary record.
Moves the root segment key to the qualified SSA key value if ROOT-SEG-KEY is numeric.
Moves the content of the child segment record to the pending authorization details record.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the program ID paragraph. It does not contain any executable statements.

This paragraph serves as the main control flow for the PAUDBLOD program. It first establishes an entry point using 'DLITCBL' and the PAUTBPCB parameter. It then calls 1000-INITIALIZE to open files and set up the program environment. After initialization, it enters a loop to read root segment records from INFILE1 using 2000-READ-ROOT-SEG-FILE until the end-of-file is reached. Subsequently, it enters another loop to read child segment records from INFILE2 using 3000-READ-CHILD-SEG-FILE until its end-of-file. Finally, it calls 4000-FILE-CLOSE to close the input files before terminating the program with GOBACK.

This paragraph initializes the program by accepting the current date and day from the system. It then displays the current date. It opens INFILE1 and INFILE2 for input. If the open operation for INFILE1 is successful (WS-INFIL1-STATUS is spaces or '00'), the program continues. Otherwise, an error message is displayed, and the program abends by calling 9999-ABEND. The same logic applies to opening INFILE2, checking WS-INFIL2-STATUS and abending if the open fails.
This paragraph is an exit point for the 1000-INITIALIZE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph reads records from INFILE1, which contains root segment data. If the read is successful (WS-INFIL1-STATUS is spaces or '00'), the INFILE1-REC is moved to PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY, and 2100-INSERT-ROOT-SEG is performed to insert the root segment into the IMS database. If the read results in an end-of-file condition (WS-INFIL1-STATUS is '10'), END-ROOT-SEG-FILE is set to 'Y'. If any other error occurs during the read, an error message is displayed.
This paragraph is an exit point for the 2000-READ-ROOT-SEG-FILE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph inserts a root segment into the IMS database using the CBLTDLI call. It uses FUNC-ISRT to specify the insert function, PAUTBPCB for the PCB, PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY as the data to insert, and ROOT-UNQUAL-SSA as the SSA. After the call, it checks the PAUT-PCB-STATUS. If the status is spaces, it displays 'ROOT INSERT SUCCESS'. If the status is 'II', it displays 'ROOT SEGMENT ALREADY IN DB'. If the status is neither spaces nor 'II', it displays 'ROOT INSERT FAILED' along with the PAUT-PCB-STATUS and performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.
This paragraph is an exit point for the 2100-INSERT-ROOT-SEG paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph reads records from INFILE2, which contains child segment data. If the read is successful (WS-INFIL2-STATUS is spaces or '00'), and if ROOT-SEG-KEY is numeric, the ROOT-SEG-KEY is moved to QUAL-SSA-KEY-VALUE, CHILD-SEG-REC is moved to PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS, and 3100-INSERT-CHILD-SEG is performed to insert the child segment into the IMS database. If the read results in an end-of-file condition (WS-INFIL2-STATUS is '10'), END-CHILD-SEG-FILE is set to 'Y'. If any other error occurs during the read, an error message is displayed.
This paragraph is an exit point for the 3000-READ-CHILD-SEG-FILE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

error_handling.condition: WS-INFIL1-STATUS NOT = SPACES OR '00' AND NOT = '10'
PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'II'
WS-INFIL2-STATUS NOT = SPACES OR '00' AND ROOT-SEG-KEY IS NOT NUMERIC

open_questions.question: What is the structure of INFILE1 and INFILE2?
open_questions.context: The copybooks for these files are not provided.
open_questions.suggestion: Locate the copybooks for INFILE1 and INFILE2 to determine their record layouts.

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of the IMS database and the data being inserted?
open_questions.context: The business context is not clear from the code.
open_questions.suggestion: Consult with the application owners to understand the purpose of the IMS database.

open_questions.question: What is the structure of the PCB (PAUTBPCB)?
open_questions.context: The copybook for PAUTBPCB is not provided.
open_questions.suggestion: Locate the copybook for PAUTBPCB to understand its structure and the meaning of the status codes.
